Everyone really wants to see Mutsumi perform again.
Well, if that's the case... What do you think, Mutsumi-chan?
M-M... Me?
I'm sure you want to see too, right, Saki? I could listen to it a hundred more times and still not get tired!
If you don't mind, we can hold the party at ours! What a coincidence, we were just talking about inviting everyone over for dinner!
Saki, what do you think?
I think... you should make the decision, Mutsumi.
...Mm. I'm okay with it.
Rowdy chatter, hearty laughs, clanking cups.
The cramped house is stuffed full with people, but nobody seems to mind the lack of space.
Alright, alright, time for another round!
Now, a warm welcome to Mutsumi-chan's pal—Sakiko!
Eyyy! Hello, Sakiko!
Everyone turns to look at her, sitting in the corner.
(Lifting her pint glass) Sakiko...?
Ch—Cheers.
Cheers—!
With her welcomed, everyone's attention returns to the party. Sakiko leans against the wall alone, her eyes a mix of emotions as she watches it all unfold.
Saki.
Mutsumi...?
Actually... I know about it all.
Huh...?
This is all fake. Minami being so nice to me. It's all fake.
Th-This—
So when I saw you, I knew, it was time to go back.
But... give me a chance.
A chance to say goodbye. Then we can end it.
Here, I can sing freely, just like everyone else.
Mutsumi...
Before Sakiko can even respond, Mutsumi picks up her guitar, and slowly walks up to the crowd.
You all ready, Mutsumi-chan?
...Hi, everyone.
What is it, Mutsumi-chan? Something wrong?
...Chair. My chair.
All ready for you. This one, yeah? The one you normally use?
Mutsumi stares intently at the stool, its seat covered in deep cracks.
Mm. That's it.
She slowly sits down. Everyone quiets their breathing.

Here goes. Please enjoy...
Haruhikage.
A melody Sakiko could never forget comes to life once more in Mutsumi's dream.
The same warm tone she remembered subtly soothes the hearts of all present.
The Mutsumi Wakaba before her is almost a stranger to Sakiko.
She is no longer the Mutsumi who struggles to express herself.
While her emotional expressions are still not very rich, here and now, she seems... so happy.

lines.st: 'A heart grown numb with cold, a trembling gaze'
'In all the world, I was alone'
'All I knew of spring was falling petals'
'And every year, my feelings are brushed off with indifference'

lines.x: So this is what you wished for, Mutsumi...
Sakiko has had countless opportunities to pull Mutsumi away from here, and she is certain Mutsumi would follow if she did.
But ultimately, she cannot bring herself to do it.
Not when this Mutsumi is so very happy.
The crowd lightly sways to the music. The feeble candlelight casts their shadows against the wall, like warm spring sunlight casting lilting shadows of trees.
Mutsumi sits on her stool, surrounded by all. Her eyes are closed, and as she strums, she gently sings.
What is on her mind in this moment?

lines.st: 'Heart-rending and endearing'
'Now that I'm here, now that I feel'
'Heartwarming and tormenting'
'That me who couldn't cry that day'
'The light gently guides that me along with it—'

lines.x: Some indescribable emotion clouds Sakiko's mind.
Is it gratification... or a refusal to accept things? Perhaps, a mix of the two.
Mutsumi Wakaba, as she is now, is much happier than when she was with Sakiko.
She sits there, alone on her stool, earnestly singing this familiar song.

lines.st: 'Shimmering and sparkling, sail through the clouds'
'My heart filled, it overflows'
'Shimmering and sparkling, down my cheeks, unnoticed'
'Burning, scorching, wetting my skin'
'Why is your hand so very warm?'
'Please, just stay like this and don't let go of my hands—'

lines.x: If she does not let go—
Then the happiness Mutsumi has found here—
Sakiko turns, and runs away in a panic.
And like that, she disappears into the night.
*pant*... *pant*... Guh...!
Muffled noises echo through the empty cavern. Somewhere nearby, excavators continue their endless digging.
The air is colder and colder.
The moment she's out of the cavern, a chill sets in.
But it merely spurs on her heart to run faster.
How... How do I face her?
She cannot word it. She cannot face it.
Tut tut tut. Whatever will you do?
Sakiko does not need to turn her head to know who it is.
Now this girl's dreamland, I'm rather satisfied with!
Sweet dreams for every child are the duty of the Castle of Dreams, and I've granted each one of her wishes.
Honestly, I'm surprised how smoothly this dream runs—no, flawlessly, in fact. Beyond my wildest expectations.
Besides, things have gotten even more interesting now, you know.
...You're deceiving her.
No, no, no, nothing of the sort. We've had plenty of dealings, she and I. She knows full well what I am.
Oh, seeing these results just fills my heart with joy.
She knows it's all a dream and she still doesn't want to leave! Isn't that the very proof my dreamspinning is unmatched?
(Chirping in agreement)
Oh, you coming here...
Now that's interesting.
Well, I'll leave you two to it, then! I trust you'll soon discover just how amusing this dream really is, Sakiko Togawa.
Sakiko. Everyone's waiting for you. Let's go back.
Go back?
Morpheus just vanished in front of you. Aren't you the least bit surprised...?
No.
Everything here, we imagined together.
'We'?
You... aren't a part of us.
You play guitar so well, Mutsumi-chan!
Saki...
No. Mum... Where did Saki go?
Saki-chan? Maybe she just went off somewhere...?
No, no. Saki... Saki would never abandon me.
The cheering crowd suddenly goes deathly quiet. They all stare straight at Mutsumi.
Mutsumi-chan, you seem to have forgotten—
This isn't just your dream.
This is 'our' dream.
'Us'.
In this dream, every 'Mutsumi' has the chance to make her wish come true.
Together with Mutsumi-chan, we've built a sweet dream that's ours and ours alone—where everyone can dream of happiness.
We exist in Mutsumi-chan's mind. We've seen her life countless times over.
In the end, we unanimously agreed that this—is the best ending for all the 'Mutsumis'—
And you, Sakiko Togawa, are the enemy of this dream.
Mutsumi, what are you talking about?
I don't want you to suffer either, but we shouldn't hide away in dreams like this—
No, Mutsumi-chan gets to live a happy life here. That's the most important thing.
I'm not like the others. I want to talk things out with you, Sakiko.
You can stay here with me, and we'll protect Mutsumi-chan's sweet dream together.
She's a fragile child. Odds are she still can't decide whether she wants to wake, or to stay here in bliss.
So long as you choose to stay as well, and help us keep this dream going, it'll naturally become 'reality'.
You are the sole determiner of 'reality' here. You are reality.
So. Stay with us, Sakiko.
I refuse.
You... refuse?
In reality, Mutsumi's body is lying out in the cold in the Castle of Dreams. Can you accept that as an ending, too?
If you truly love her, you would not be doing this to her.
As long as Mutsumi-chan is happy, as long as we can exist—what does it matter what's reality or not?!
I have Mutsumi-chan's every memory! I know your heart's deepest desire!
If you'll just stay here, I can even give you a whole, entire Ave Mujica!
Shut up! You're not Mutsumi at all... She would never say that sort of thing.
I like you, and I like Mutsumi-chan...
Why are you forcing me to choose like this?

lines.st: 'Do it.'

lines.x: An unidentified voice hovers above their heads.

lines.st: 'Do it, do it, do it.'

lines.x: The distant mountains seem to shake. The gravel on the ground jostles to a rhythm.

lines.st: 'Do it.'

lines.x: There's no one around to be seen, but Sakiko can almost feel countless gazes boring into her.

lines.st: 'Do it—!'

lines.x: They're right. To Mutsumi-chan, we are her countless shadows.
So that the shadows may hide forever in the darkness, even the weakest light must be snuffed out.
Step by tiny step, 'Mutsumi' forces Sakiko towards a precipice.
...What are you doing?!
Sakiko...
'Mutsumi' reaches out, placing her hand on Sakiko's shoulder.
Sakiko loses her balance. 'Mutsumi's' figure grows smaller and smaller.
I want Mutsumi-chan to be happy forever, Sakiko. I also don't want the 'Mutsumis' to have to disappear anymore.
I just have no choice...
Mutsumi-chan... doesn't need you.

lines.st: 'Please, just stay like this and don't let go of my hands—'
'Please don't ever, ever let me go.'

lines.x: Goodbye forever, Sakiko.
A cold wind whistles past my ears.
Down I fall, faster and faster.
But there is no end to this abyss.
My crazy ideas? You're the only drummer I know who can actually make them happen.
You were the most hardworking drummer I have ever met.
All you had to do... was be yourself, and nothing more.
You kept hopping from group to group, yet you never found a place to truly belong... Is this really the life you want?
You should never have had to bear this pain...
Umiri, let's get out of here. Together.
I will find you. I have to...
Uika...
Mutsumi, none of this is real. We need to...
BANG!
Her heart rate is ragged, her breathing is out of control, and her back is soaked with sweat.
Sakiko's consciousness has returned to her body once more, albeit in this most awful, unbecoming manner.
Felt real, didn't it?
Falling straight to the ground from who knows how high up.
Oh, and then when the dream shattered and you were trapped in dreamless Limbo... how did that feel?
No matter how sweet those dreams were, they have to come to an end.
You pry into their most fragile parts and dramatize them over and over...
Must say, I don't agree.
Before you barged into their dreams uninvited, they'd all but forgotten about you and your insignificant little band.
Ave Mujica is our contract. They entrusted their lives to me—
You were the only one who ever cared about it!
What do you—?!
Heard it for yourself, didn't you?
One after another, they all rejected you.
Not a chance. Everyone here loves me for the actress I am.
I gave everything I had for this... and now you're telling me it's all fake?
What a load of crap. I'm not accepting that!
This is my dream, and I don't need anyone else in it, thanks!
How can you possibly understand me?
This lifestyle is perfect... It suits me better.
I don't need someone to save me.
And no one could save me.
I knew it. Mutsumi-chan didn't need Sakiko.
Hahahah-hah... Hah-hahahahah!!!
I got to see every one of your sorriest, most helpless, most laughable moments.
You may have destroyed the dreams I spun, but I suppose this makes us even.
And I have to thank you. You roamed their dreams again and again...
In the process, proving to me that the dreamlands I wove were perfect, impeccable.
And now, even that pest ought to admit it. It's me. I'm the most talented of us all in the Castle of Dreams.
'Pest'?
Didn't you meet her in your dream?
The snooty girl? Flowery high-nobility frock? All the outdated pleasantries?
Just being around her puts me ill at ease.
Iris is this place's master. You are nothing but a fraud!
She said it! No matter how sweet the dream, one has to wake up in the end—
Nngh—!
Sakiko feels her vocal cords freeze in place. She cannot make even the slightest peep.
You know what? I finally understand why you get on my nerves so much.
You're just like her. You never listen to what others think. You just sit there spouting big, empty words forever.
You say they entrusted their lives to you, but let's be fair. You just don't want to be abandoned by others. Isn't that right?
I've seen your life. There's not much to it. Just a princess's stubborn struggle after her fall from grace.
Each and every action of yours was done to deceive and persuade yourself, and to present yourself as never having fallen.
You're self-important, conceited, and an utter lost cause.
What exactly does your band entail? Hmph. Just a bunch of girls using a so-called contract to bind one another for fun.
And now that they've all found happiness, of course they're abandoning you. Poor Sakiko Togawa. All alone once more.
If you really believe what Iris said, then fine, I'll prepare another round of the sweetest dreams for you.
It'll be perfect, impeccable. Realize it's a farce all you want, you still won't want to wake up.
Like your bandmates, you'll wallow in your dreams, unable to ever pluck yourself out.
Nngh—!
Something to say? Alright, you get one last time. Speak your mind.
The impish girl draws a pattern in the air, and there is a sensation of chains falling away.
I—
Then, the chains are pulled tight once again.
Hahahah-hah-hah!
I knew it. You are much cuter when you're not talking!
As Sakiko struggles, Morpheus paces closer, step by step. Out reaches her right hand, and she places it on Sakiko's cheek.
O ye, Princess of Hypocrisy. While you were on your dream tour, I've come to completely understand you as a person.
What you thirst for the most, what you need the most, and what you fear the most.
But don't worry. This dream? Will be absolutely perfect. You'll be more awake than ever. Not that you'll ever want to wake up.
Now sleep, my noble, selfish, conceited little doll girl.
Sweet dreams.
Clank, clunk.
Sakiko? Sakiko!
Mm... hmm?
I am NOT the kind of person you claim I—
Sakiko bursts out yelling, but she is no longer in the castle, and Morpheus is nowhere to be found.
You okay? You look terrible. You have another nightmare?
I'm... back in my dream again.
What are you talking about? Didn't you just wake up?
What, you sleep so long your brain's fuzzy?
Iris and Bena are here...
Er, how do you do... I don't believe I introduced myself?
I think she was already asleep when we got on?
Vigna and I aren't late... The dream has changed...
Sakiko, you know, you keep saying some pretty weird stuff—

lines.st: We are heading to a small town in Victoria to find a girl named Ann who is acquainted with Iris.
This vehicle will have a flat tire soon. We will be forced to get off to fix the tire, delaying us by a few hours...

lines.x: Are we going to Victoria to find Ann?
Who's Ann? Do you know, Iris?
I don't. Is that a friend of yours who lives there?
What...? You don't know her, Iris?
Then Vigna and I...
Uh, hello? We're on a field mission not too far away from your home. I figured I'd bring you back for a break, let you see your family.
You've been mega busy with work. Gotta be a while since the last time you went home, right?
What's going on? Why did the car suddenly stop?!
Did a tire burst?!
*huff*... *huff*... *pant*... Phew, we almost hit a burdenbeast!
That was close, but no worries. We can be back on the road right away!
As the engine starts humming again, the view outside the window begins to move once more.
The same wilderness, and the same companions.
That said, this dream is starting to bare its own colors.
Iris! Bena!
I brought you all sweets, just like I promised! Everyone gets a fair share!
Yayyy!!!
Now, did you keep your promise too? Have you figured out what you treasure the most?
I treasure my beastie bank! Look at all the coins I've saved!
I planted these flowers myself, they finally sprouted... They're so pretty, and I was wondering if you could preserve them.
Okay, okay, one at a time! Line up, everybody!
Iris and Bena try to have each child introduce their treasures in an orderly manner, but it seems every one of them is exuding limitless passion.
The children's excited voices don't die down for a second, but there's no chaos in Sakiko's eyes. On the contrary, the sight before her is filled with warmth.
Why, if it isn't Sakiko.
What brings you home so suddenly?
Sorry, who...?
We just happened to have a field mission in the area, so I figured I'd bring Sakiko along, get her to pay her family a visit while she's here.
Your father's been talking about you every day, you know? He says you've been so busy interning at a large company, you hardly ever write home.
I've never seen him that talkative. He must really miss you!
You should hurry home. Give him a nice surprise, and lift that weight off his shoulders!
(Whispers) Sakiko, you've been acting kinda weird... Like, your reactions are kinda slow.
Um... Pardon me, how do I get home from here?
Though the conversation that follows is incredibly embarrassing, Sakiko follows the kind townsperson's instructions and successfully makes her way 'back'.
Following the meandering path, she at last arrives at the door of what is allegedly her home. A white, strange building.

The flowers and grass in the courtyard are well maintained. She can hear the flowerbeds' sprinklers quietly watering the plants around them. The chimney lets out puffs of smoke, as the master of the house prepares lunch.
But she knows that this return is not to her real home.
Her real home is in a faraway, unreachable place.
Home. The first thing that comes to her mind is the Togawa family mansion, followed by the dusty, cramped little place her father rented that always smells of alcohol.
She thinks about how hardworking her father once was.

Alas, her real father is probably wandering the streets with a bottle of booze right now. He might even be uttering her name, though the sorry excuses he gives alongside are the furthest thing from 'sweet'.
Right. That is Sakiko Togawa's reality.
What she sees before her is a dream spun for her by Morpheus, and it doubles as a cage to imprison her.
Suddenly, the aroma of food seizes her.
Hmm-hmm... ♪ Hmm-hm-hmm... ♪ Hmm-hmm... ♪ Hmm-hm-hmm... ♪
...Sakiko?!
What brings you back all of a sudden? I thought you were busy with work?
I am... We have a field mission, so I thought I would drop by.
You've hardly ever written home! Every day, I've been reading the few letters you did send over and over.
They're no more than simple letters. You needn't...
How could they be no more than letters when they're written by my dear daughter?!
Father, I think something's burning...
Just hold on!
I lost track of the time... I'll have to remake it from scratch...
I was taking classes for a good while to learn to make your favorite dishes, Sakiko. How about a taste?
Father? I thought you never used to cook.
I have a lot more time on my hands nowadays, so I thought I might as well try something new.
And look at you... How much weight have you lost...
We have regular medical evaluations at Rhodes Island—I weigh just as much as I did before.
No, you've definitely lost some weight!
With a worried look on his face, Sakiko's 'father' reaches a hand out to pat his daughter's head.
Huh...?
Her father's hand feels at once unfamiliar and nostalgic.
As she remembers it, she would always be stooping down, her drunken father's arm over her shoulder, as they slowly inched homeward one step at a time.
His hand hung ever so feebly before her eyes, as close as it was just now, but so, so different...
I'm not a child anymore. Please don't...
Well, you'll always be my most darling daughter.
Don't want you forgetting your smile, not even while you're out working.
...Yes.
The two of them smile at each other. The hesitation in Sakiko's mind is cast aside.
She's certain that this smile of hers, right now, is true and genuine.
I can hear piano from upstairs... Do we have a guest today?
Hm? What are you talking about, Sakiko?
Surely that's Mizuho practicing the piano.
...Mother?
Well, yes! It's always been you and Mizuho who know how to play it.
Oh, right, I haven't told Mizuho you're back yet.
She's going to be overjoyed to see you. You should head on up!
Sakiko's legs begin to shake.
And a low hum continues echoing in her mind.
Piano.
As the music continues, her memories of her mother slowly begin to well forth in her heart.
She still remembers how she cried her eyes out on the day of her mother's funeral.
The piano was her mother's favorite instrument. That is why Sakiko loves the piano.
Sakiko's mother would hold Sakiko's hands so gently, and teach her to feel her way around each and every key, black and white.
In the days following her mother's death, Sakiko could still vividly recall every moment of her time spent with her mother, and kept playing song after song her mother had taught her.
Run.
All of this is fake. A dream spun from yarn by Morpheus.
That is not her real mother.
How could Morpheus possibly know everything about her?
She is nothing but a crude forgery.
And yet.
And yet—
Her legs do not carry her to a stop. They seem to move faster, if anything.
Despite this being a dream. Despite her being a 'forgery'.
Because just like that, she truly is right there.
Sitting there, completely engrossed in her performance.
A ray of light filters in from the window and drapes her figure, making her somewhat hard to make out.
But at this moment, only one thing is on Sakiko's mind.
M... Mother...?
Her 'mother' stops playing, and turns around.
...Sakiko?
Everything before her eyes is so, so real.

lines.st: A fowlbeast that flies for its whole life does so not out of an unwillingness to land.
Its decisive wings shroud the pain and tragedy that come with having no legs.
I do not fear forgetting.

lines.x: You've gotten so good at the piano!
D-Do you think so?
I know better than anyone how much you've changed. I'm your mother, after all.
I can hear it all—how hard you've been working... I know your job must be so hectic, and yet you've kept up with your piano practice.
Huh?
It seems you've gone through quite a lot out there...
Hate to disturb you two while you're practicing! Supper's ready?
Mm—this is wonderful!
What do you think? My cooking's really improved, eh?
You don't know the half of it, Sakiko... He had me taste-test so much in order to finally get to this point.
Hurry and give this salad a try, hm?
Mmh! Delicious!
I've made a lot of minor adjustments so it's more to your liking! Not half bad, eh?
And there's plenty more dishes you love on the way!
Yep!
Ngh... Ugh...
Sakiko? Sakiko!
Wh-What's wrong?
I think—I've had a little too much to eat...
Are you feeling better now...?
Sorry, I think I overdid it a little...
Honestly, your father never changes...
But that's his style, I suppose. Honestly, most times it's quite adorable of him.
You'll forgive him, won't you?

lines.st: Can I truly forgive him?
When the man who shares his face has broken my heart countless times?

lines.x: He really loves you, you know.
And so do I.
I really love the both of you.
Mother, where is this coming from...?
I've been going through our photo albums more and more while you were away.
I started from when you were born... then when you were one, then two, then three...
My childhood memory nearly failed me...
Maybe. But there's nothing more important, nothing more special to a parent than their child.
When you were born, your palm wasn't even half as big as mine.
But look at you now. Yours is even a little bigger.
Sakiko's mother takes her hand and brings their palms together.
For some reason beyond herself, Sakiko grasps her mother's hand and locks fingers with hers.
Mother, your hand is so warm...

lines.st: Was this how warm her palm is?
Is this all real? Is everything I'm enjoying right now rational, reasonable?
How can it be this warm, and yet this heartrending?

lines.x: That's right. I'm right here.
Mother...
Go on. You can cry all you want. I always comforted you like this when you cried.
Huh? Was I... really such a crybaby?
When you were very, veeery little. Oh, you would make the biggest fuss over a single sweet.
You may be a strong young woman now, but you were a big crybaby back then.
R-Really? I don't remember at all...
*giggle* Your mother would never lie to you.
Then... Do you remember my high school entrance ceremony?

lines.st: During my last year of middle school, my mother died of illness.
Even if they're nonexistent memories, even if they're things that never happened.
But right now, all I want is the most beautiful answers I can get.
I want to convince myself, to create the most wonderful memories, to believe in the sweetest dream I can.

lines.x: Of course. I remember you in your school uniform. Our whole family took a photo together at the school gate.
Yes, that sounds right... Oh, that does bring me back.
What else have we done together? Mother... I want you to tell me. Tell me about all the memories we share.
Sakiko, just how long have you been away from home? Aren't you the clingy one today?
Home...

lines.st: When did the word 'home' start sounding so foreign to me?

lines.x: Hmm... Well, if you want, I'll reminisce a little more.
Now where should I begin?
Let's start from when I was born. I want to hear it all.
Okay, then we'll start from the day you were born...
And so, down the country path, at a leisurely pace, Sakiko and her mother walk side-by-side, and it feels as if they could walk forever.
Just like that sweet, beautiful stretch of time they shared back when it all began. Unhurried and without end.
Their hands, the umbilical cord that connected them in the very beginning.
Sakiko—
You found your way home!
Miss Vigna, this carriage seems to be out of order. Now let me down!
Let's see... W—Wait, these controls aren't like what I learned at Rhodes Island!
Oh, crap, crap, crap, we can't stop!
We're going to crash!
Sakiko, out of the way—!
Mother, would you mind waiting a moment?
Of course. Go ahead.
Girls, are you alright?!
Nngh... I'm okay. Butt hurts a little, but I'm fine...
I swear I saw Miss Iris too just now...
Mmf, mrrmrmff!
A familiar voice can be heard from deep within a hay pile.
Geh... Quick, we gotta dig Iris out!
R-Right!
(Gasping for breath) I... I almost suffocated!
Weren't you two collecting children's wishes? What are you doing here?
Hee-hee! It all went better than expected. The children here are surprisingly good, you know, so we wrapped up ahead of time.
I thought I'd give driving in the countryside a shot, but then car trouble hit while I was at the wheel, and...
Not my first time repairing vehicles like this one. Hurry, let's fix this one up together.
*sigh* I can hardly tell if my luck is supposed to be good or rotten today...
I couldn't shake the worry something might happen, you know.
I didn't expect our work to proceed so well today, and I certainly didn't expect this little mishap.
I only hope we can have this fixed soon... At least then we can be on our way back by tonight.
Tonight...?
Sakiko turns to look behind her.
Not too far away, Sakiko's mother is watching her and her friends with a smile.
She notices her daughter's glance, and gives a light nod.
Would that be... your mother?
...Yes.
You very much resemble one another, especially the eyes and the mouth! I feel both a gentle warmth and a firm resolution in you.
But I... I have no resolve.
What makes you say that?
Miss Iris, you're a fairy, if I'm not mistaken?
Yes... Is something wrong?
Fairies listen to people's wishes, and then temporarily make them 'reality' in their dreams... correct?
...Correct.
But if—if people are well and truly trapped in their dreams, never to pluck themselves out, what should be done then?
A real fairy would never permit that to happen in the first place.
If you spend too long in a dream, you lose the ability to discern between dream and reality.
What if they never wake up?

Sakiko, the worst outcome has never been the inability to wake up. Rather, it is when you accept everything in your dream as the truth, and then you wake, only to find that all you have experienced was a fleeting mirage.
After living a lifetime of happiness in the dream, you find yourself back in reality, back at the very beginning again.
Your body is young, but your soul is old. Your dream is well beyond your grasp. You are left with no choice but to carry on in life, utterly alone.
Can you really call that a good dream?
If it is doomed to fail, it should not be fled from in dreams; if they are destined to be missed, you must bid them farewell properly.
Sakiko looks back again.
Oh, I appear to have gone on a little long... Am I interrupting your reunion with your mother?
...No, nothing of the sort!
I apologize. Someone very important to me asked me the same question, once upon a time.
Because of my inability to give a firm answer then, things turned irrecoverable.
Hence, I now lend such concern to this...
Heeey—!
The car's all fixed up!
Vigna, no more touching! No touching ever!
Guh...
C'mon, girls, get on—
Miss Sakiko, will you be coming back with us?
Sakiko turns once more to look at her 'mother'.
She's still standing there, as though she's awaiting something.
Is she awaiting Sakiko's promise? Or perhaps Sakiko's verdict?
She simply stands there, looking on at her daughter with the warmest smile.
It's okay, Sakiko. You can stay here a little longer. You've been worn out lately, yeah? You're due for a vacation anyway. I'll let HR know.
Okay... Thank you.
Let me try again—
Sakiko, let's stop here for now.
Aren't you tired? I'll make us some tea, then we can take our time—
I'm just nervous, that's all—
How about a little chat, then?
Were those girls just now friends of yours?
Yes. They are my coworkers at Rhodes Island, and very good friends of mine.
Rhodes Island...
Correct. A pharmaceutical company, boasting a landship of tremendous scale.
Aboard the ship, we live and work, sailing anywhere and everywhere in the world.
The... 'world'?
...Terra.
What an interesting word for it!
Does Rhodes Island have a piano room you can use?
It does. There isn't too much entertainment while the ship is sailing. Many operators while away the time by playing instruments.
At times, I'll play a few songs on the keyboard in my dormitory room. Vigna has even formed her own band—
And have you formed your own band, too?
I have.
Is that with Miss Vigna?
No. It's with others...
Well, now I'm curious, Sakiko. What are these fellow band members of yours like?
I have a drummer who constantly clashes with me over just about everything, though her ambition and hardworking nature are second to none. We resemble sisters-in-arms more than we do friends.
I have a bassist whose technique is superb. She handles all our behind-the-scenes work, schedules included, with great diligence. As a colleague, there could be no one more trustworthy.
I have a guitarist-vocalist who excels at songwriting. She has her own work as an idol to take care of, but in spite of how busy it makes her, she never ceases to support me.
And I have another guitarist—we've been friends since childhood. She... she was always there for me, even when I left home and was all alone...
By the sound of it, you're quite the close-knit band.
No, the reality is we constantly argue. We even disbanded before.
Have you all made up since?
I got separated from everyone.
They are somewhere far, far away. Somewhere I cannot reach.
How far away? From Victoria to Ursus? Or even farther than that?
Why the hesitation?
Because... Because...
...Because I'm afraid that finding them means leaving you behind.
I'm scared I won't be able to see you again—
Silly girl. I'll always be here.
But—didn't you leave me behind?!

lines.st: 'Ma... Mama...'
'Look, dear, Sakiko is calling out to me...'
'Mama... Mama...'
'Sakiko, Sakiko! Call for Daddy too!'
'Pa... Mama...'
'Aaaaaaagghhhh!'
'Good. This key first, then this key...'
'Wow! Now I can play the same sounds as you, Mother!'
'That's right! You're so amazing, Sakiko!'
'I want to keep learning the piano with you, Mother! I want to be with you all the time!'
'...Yes. We'll always be together.'
'Mizuho-chan... she left us too soon...'
'With a condition like hers, we'd be lucky to last as long as she did.'
'Mother...'
'Sakiko, I'll do everything I can for you.'
'Father... why do you drink so much?'
'Sakiko... I'll do everything I can...'
'I can't... do this anymore.'
'Father, Mother would be so sad if she saw you like this!'
'Didn't she tell you how...'
'Shut up!'
'Sakiko, I'm begging you... Just leave me be.'
Mother, I know... I've always known.
I shouldn't be here. This is not my world.
The sweetest dreams cannot last forever. The day will come when I have to leave you.
And yet—and yet—
How I wish—the time for farewell would come a little later...

lines.x: The warmth of her body... The realness of her breathing.
Sakiko's mother gazes warmly upon her.
I'm sorry, Sakiko... Mother lied to you.
Huh...?
You knew, didn't you? That I wasn't your real mother.
I'm sorry. I've been so careful to keep this little white lie going.
I wasn't there at your high school entrance ceremony. Before you asked me that question, my memory of you was still in middle school.
And I don't know why it is, but when I see you smile these days, there are dark clouds hanging above you...
Sakiko. When did you start making this kind of face?
Just what suffering have you been through, in this stretch of time I know nothing about?
I'm sorry. I don't know anything about this. All I know is that during your hardest, most painful moments, I wasn't there for you.
Sakiko, I am so, so truly happy that I get to see you.
I really, really want to see you grow up.
And I want to stand right there in front of the stage and see your live shows.
And I want to meet those new friends of yours.
I'm very greedy. I want to know everything about you, and be there by your side.
But because I'm your mother... and because I love you so much...
I want you to leave this place, and seek out your real life.
Go, Sakiko.
Go back to your own 'world'.
Mother...!
The emotion welling up in Sakiko's heart at last overflows. She buries her head in her mother's chest and bursts into tears.
In contrast, as she has always done, Sakiko's mother simply strokes her back and consoles her.
Huh? Sakiko, are you off again?
Sakiko has something important to do.
Oh, you just got home. Can't you stay a little longer...?
Dear...
Alright!
Sakiko, your mother and I are on the same page!
(Breaking up) Just... Just... remember to eat right while you're away from home!
Right. I will.
Okay... I'll be off now.
Sure!
Have a safe journey, Sakiko—
From the rooftop, dozens of stories high, the cars and people on the ground are like specks of dust.
We've strengthened security around your home, Miss Nyamu. Until the situation is under control, please refrain from going out if at all possible.
With the kidnapper still at large, the agency prefers you scale back your activities for the time being out of safety concerns.
Scale back my activities...?
Just temporarily. We're sifting through new prospective partners to ensure your absolute safety.
But my fans...
Miss Nyamu, you are now one of Lungmen's biggest stars. You need to recognize your own popularity a bit more.
But...!
Use this time to get some well-deserved rest, or do something you enjoy.
—Just stay put.
I'm going to hang up now. The L.G.D. wants to talk with us again.
Stay put, stay home, huh...?
Nyamu looks around her home—
If it could even be called one.
The massive TV in the living room, which only ever has one viewer.
Someone comes to make the bed every day. It never looks anything but factory new.
Suddenly, something catches her eye.
Found you...
Perhaps it was not that she had forgotten them; perhaps she just did not want to look for them.
At some point, she chose to abandon them of her own accord.
A set of drumsticks, lying in a corner of the bookshelf.
(Tapping drumsticks together) ...One, two, three, four.
...Wow, that was awful.
You let her go.
You defied my orders.
I broke the terms of our contract this time. Here is the penalty fee.
Umiri pulls out a bag of gold coins, and places it on the table in front of her boss.
...You're rejecting me.
I think it would be best if we stayed hirer and contractor.
I work better as a lone agent. If I want a partner, I have my bass.
No. You want to go back to Francis and his people.
I've had quite a longstanding interest in their musical parties. Sorry, but I won't be taking a seat at your bigger-stakes table.
Hahahaha... When did I ever say you'd have a seat?
You've only ever been a chip.
And a chip not in the hand is worth nothing.
With those words, the door slams open, and Umiri finds herself surrounded by multiple burly brawlers.
Umiri turns back to look at the boss, and is greeted by the glinting end of a barrel.
The island is about to disappear into the distance.
What's wrong? You've been staring at the island this whole time.
I thought I heard someone calling to me...
Haha! Must be hearing things. Nothing in these waters except the sound of waves and fowl.
(Glances) Lotta luggage there. Headed to the city to strike it rich?
...I'm looking for someone.
Ain't that the damnedest thing. You're not the first one to say that...
What...?
(Loudly) These waves are getting rough...
Above them, darkness has started to creep into the sky.
(Even louder) Watch out... grab... railing...
But it is too late.
A wave crashes down on the deck, and sends seawater flooding into the cabins.
Wrong... wrong... wrong!
This guitar sounds... wrong.
But she knows why full well. A classical guitar could never make the sound of an electric.
(Quietly) It's awful late, Mutsumi-chan. Won't you get some sleep?
(Quietly) Look, at least just unlock the door...
(Quietly) We'll sit by your side and tell you bedtime stories, just like old times.
Not unless you tell me... where Saki went.
Well, we don't know either...
Maybe she went off and found her own happiness, somewhere else?
...You're lying.
Saki's not like that.
There is no response from outside the door.
Saki won't change so easily. She wants me to return to Ave Mujica—
You mustn't change so easily either, Mutsumi-chan.
Somehow, Mutsumi's mother is standing behind her.
This isn't just your dream. It's ours too.
We are all Mutsumi.
This is our dream, together. Where we can all truly exist, where we all find happiness.
Mutsumi-chan, you don't need Sakiko. You only need us.
No...
Let's be—together—forever—and ever—okay?
No—!
Ten minutes left...
Bye, Miss Iris! Bye, Miss Bena—
Bye-bye, bye now!
You be good children, just like you promised!
We will—
Iris, Bena, all set? In that case, let's—
Wait!
Miss Sakiko...?
I thought you wouldn't come...
*pant*... *pant*...
Iris... the necklace...
N-Necklace?
In your left pocket—the necklace in the little box that keeps the nightmares away!
What are you talking about, Miss Sakiko? I don't... what?
Iris reflexively reaches into her pocket, and is surprised to indeed find something in there.
Sakiko... you don't actually have precognition, do you?
Center stage. Mutsumi lies in a pool of blood.
My child! Oh, my child!
Help her! Somebody!
What's wrong? Is she hurt?
Poor girl! She's dead! How horrible!
It's all a trick! She's not dead. How could she be dead?
(Noisy chirping)
(Excited chirping)
Cut!
...You're an abysmal actor, Wolff.
Sorry. Acting's not my forte.
(Merry laughter)
Oh, and you think YOU were any better?!
(Panicked crying)
This won't do. I'm still short of actors... I need one more...
Morpheus stares daggers at the center of the stage. There, a blue-haired girl sits, eyes closed, but brow furrowed as ever in struggle.
(Dismayed chirping)
It's a Siracusan play. Caused quite the controversy, in its opening run.
True and false, false and true, why does everyone tie themselves in knots over which is which?
Human beings have too strong a need to know everything down to the core.
Perhaps, it's the very thing that makes them human.
I'm sorry. I spoke out of line again.
No... Wolff. You're fluent now.
Maybe it's all the rehearsals lately... Then again, maybe it's not the rehearsals, but the things I've been seeing. The sort of things that grow a heart a size or two.
Can a woven creation have a 'heart'?
The books tell of a metal man who gained one. Perhaps I can too.
Tut. Shouldn't have let you read so much... I liked your stuttering better.
She has no 'heart' anymore?!
I can patch her back up, but without a heart, she'll never leave this place again...
You decide... decide Morpheus's fate.
(Oh, Iris... oh, Bena...)
Alas. It would be better if you didn't talk.
If only, if only, everyone was like me...
Morpheus. It's time we ended this farce of yours.
Upon the stage, where a doll slumbered, now a doll stands.
What? Excuse me? How?
This isn't right! No, how could you—
I made myself clear. I will not be leaving my companions until I have awoken them.
Well, I'll give you two choices—
One! Get out of my sight, right now.
Two! I trap you under layers and layers and LAYERS of dreams, and you stay lost in Limbo forever.
I decline.
You think you can—?!
Sakiko reaches into her pocket.
(Yes. It's still there.)
Iris's necklace has accompanied her through layer after layer of dreams, and it has followed her here.
Now, she will save Ave Mujica. She will save everyone.
Our bet is still on.
In their dreams, I saw their innermost thoughts.
Even as their wishes came true one by one, they found no happiness in it.
They are no hypocrites for it. They are simply still in doubt.
As the founder of Ave Mujica, I pledged to them that I would take responsibility for their lives.
From the moment I uttered those words, our fates became inseparable...
My role is to show the path to lost dolls. I will lead them through the fog, for I must.
Center stage, Sakiko Togawa stands. She casts her eye over her slumbering companions, then returns her gaze to Morpheus.
They, and you... are no more than lost dolls.
In the moonlight, the doll takes on a temporal life.
Awaken—
Stop her!
(Terrifying roar)
This is our dream. Therefore, we are its Master.
We will become as we want to be!
Sakiko Togawa slowly raises a hand, and her clothing begins to transform.
She recalls the Originium Arts she learned in her dream; the songs she wanted to write.
As she has practiced countless times before, she casts down a firm finger—
Upon a white piano key.
The note rings clear in every corner on and off the stage, and bids the threads holding up each doll fall slack.
No longer puppeted, they fall to the expanse of the stage one by one.
Be assured that in the face of all your efforts, I can hardly rest on my own laurels.
Despite all our hardships, we came back together and put on a masquerade that belonged to us, didn't we?
I want to write a song—
About our struggles, and the possibilities of the future.
We are a band. Our destinies are one.
This is my answer.
Put down the guitar, Mutsumi-chan.
You're our princess. We'll be by your side, whatever you do.
—Cover her ears!
(Hugs guitar tightly)
You're not me...
I miss Saki...
Somebody, help...
Honestly, what do I do with you?
The 'Mutsumis' throwing themselves at Mutsumi suddenly crash to the ground one by one, as if marionettes whose strings were cut.
I'm on your side, Mutsumi-chan. I'll listen to your deepest desire.
The fully-armed brawlers are closing in on Umiri.
Time we put an end to this, Umiri...
What? What's this song...?
AAAAAAAAAARGH!!!
Umiriiiii!
Francis...?! Where did you just drop in from?!
Sakiko told us all about it! We're all just figments of your dream, ain't we?
Why would you go and believe her nonsense...
No! Don't you see, everything makes sense now!
We trusted you unconditionally because you made us!
Now that I've accepted it, I feel so light, so free. Umiri, I could do ANYTHING right now!
There isn't much time! You've gotta get outta this nightmare!
But how am I going to...?
It's your dream! You've gotta know!
But once I leave, you're all going to...
When'd you start getting so hung up on crap?
Don't you worry! Me and my brothers, we'll think of you when we hold our parties!
...Got it!
Go!
Don't let her get away—!
Umiri grabs her bass guitar case by the handle, bends her knees and lowers her center of gravity—
I'm—outta here!
Umiri, our sister! Buon viaggio!
Miss Nyamu...!
A song that none of them have ever heard is blaring at maximum volume.
Yet there is nobody in the room.
Call the security firm!
S-She started streaming!
Good morn-nyamu-nyamu, everyone! Nyamuchi here~!
Your girl's soaring through the skies of Lungmen right now! Haha, and I've got a LOT of patrol cars behind me in hot pursuit!
Whoooo! Feeling good!! Now this is one way to remember Nyamuchi the rest of your lives, huh?
And if you do get to hold onto any memories, remember one thing—
Nyamuchi is the drummer of Ave Mujica—Amoris!
(My memories... they're fading, piece by piece...)
(The last trace I had of her... it's being erased...)
(That song... that song...)
(A song of the soul...)
(I say... you're mine... you're mine...)
(Look... you're so close, yet still...)
(Light...)
(You're like light...)
(You always had the power to make people gather around you, basking in your light and warmth...)
(That's who you are... Saki-chan.)
Performing, shouting your heart out, again and again, never to stop.
...I apologize.
I apologize, for the countless times I've hurt you all.
But here and now, I want to see you more than anyone—I want to see you as you really are.
The destinies of Ave Mujica are one. I bear the weight of your lives on my shoulders, and so I will stake my life to protect you.
I beg you... all of you, wake up.
Midnight. The twin moons hang high in the night sky. Now is the hour of the dolls' awakening.
Let us commence the world of Ave Mujica anew.
The ending note settles. Sakiko's hand lingers on the keyboard.
Faint echoes of music reverberate on through the empty castle halls.
Saki... chan...?
From beside comes a voice long unheard, yet one she could not know any more intimately. She slowly lifts her head.
We finally... finally...
...We finally got to meet, Saki-chan.
It feels like I had this long, long dream... and I was this close to never waking up.
Saki-chan, we've been going in circles for so long. We almost missed each other again.
And before Sakiko stands not just Uika, but her entire band.
It was like someone gave me a pull, and then I just showed up here.
Blehhh! Why are we being so serious?
But Saki, you're smiling so happily.
Sakiko has to squint beneath the bright spotlight hanging above. Everything before her feels so unreal, so dreamlike.
But she knows, she knows—
Once more, they have played the music that belongs to them.
The girls of Ave Mujica have finally come back.
Yes! Of course I am, I'm so happy...
Uika... Mutsumi.
Nyamu... Umiri.
I missed you... I missed you so badly.
All of you—welcome back.
(Struggling chirping)
I'm guessing these things put us to sleep?
The dreams... they're not working...
No... no...
You can't leave... you have to... stay here!
(Piercing screech)
—Watch out!
Wolff!!
Morpheus, cut your losses!
Don't you lecture me with words you cribbed from a storybook!
Sakiko, take them and—argh!
Before Wolff can finish, an army of threads skewers through its arms, expelling the cotton within.
Oh, crap! Up there—
Countless Corettons circle above, weaving a massive dreamcatcher.
It begins to press in towards the girls, growing ever larger and larger.
Morpheus... see the error of your ways!
A huge pair of scissors falls from the sky, shredding the dream web into naught but tiny wisps of silk that drift in the air.
(Panicked screech)
Who was that?!
Nngh—!
The girl gives a few twitches in midair before plummeting to the ground, faster and faster—
Are... are we safe now?
What in the world is happening...?
Apologies for our lateness.
M-Miss Iris! We owe a debt to you for your aid... Thank you.
Sakiko is overcome with relief at seeing the familiar fairy. Her exhaustion swiftly catches up, and she feels the strength drain from her as she collapses.
A dull thud echoes through the hall.
Saki-chaaaan!
Sakiko... Sakiko...
(Who is it... now...)
Bit by bit, Sakiko regains consciousness, and is greeted by a faint fragrance.
Her eyes flutter open, and she finds herself lying on a soft velvet sofa, a brown-haired girl sitting by her side.
Ah. Have you come to?
You fainted. Going through dreamland after dreamland like that takes a toll on the body.
Though I'd say it's quite incredible you lasted that long despite never encountering such Originium Arts before.
Are the others...
You needn't fret, I've arranged rooms for them. Bena is with them right now.
Oh, I don't believe I've introduced myself—I am Iris, the present Master of the Castle of Dreams.
We've seen plenty of each other in my dreams... If this is our first time meeting, then who were you before?
Well, as Morpheus demonstrated for you—some fairies of the Castle of Dreams possess the rare Arts to spin dreams.
That said, it's no omnipotent thing. What dreams a caster spins are ultimately limited by their own knowledge of the target.
Morpheus didn't understand you, nor did she regard you as an equal adversary. That is why the dream she spun was so flawed.
As for why you met me, I would suppose... that was Grandmother's work.
Grandmother?
Yes. The Castle of Dreams's previous Master. She spends all her time in a secluded room to convalesce now, owing to her frail health.

She gave me this necklace, you see, and over the last few days, it had been terribly temperamental with its energies. Bena and I scrambled to return just in case something was amiss... What a terrible turn of affairs we found.
I see...
Something just started up over there. You're not safe alone. Go the long way round, quick.
...Thank you for the warning.
I heard from an old woman nearby...
You should hurry home.
The nights are dark here. Now don't you get lost again.
Miss Sakiko. As the Master of the Castle of Dreams, I beg your forgiveness for all that's transpired.
I suppose you could hardly pardon Morpheus, after all she's done...?
Actually... By all rights I should hate her, but I don't feel that way at all.
...What?
She was quite right, actually. Ave Mujica as a group was never very tightly-knit.
When I made that initial bet with her, it was solely because I wanted the band members back. It was an act of rash judgment.
But after entering their dreams only to be rejected by each of them in turn, it finally dawned on me that I hadn't a clue how they felt inside.
Within the dreams she'd spun, we tried to hide our weakest, most fragile parts beneath a beautiful facade.
Yet did I ever attempt to know my companions, with whom I'd spent so many days and nights?
Had I truly resolved to bear the weight of their lives?
As I fell through the abyss of Limbo, these were the questions I asked myself in infinitude.
Miss Sakiko...
But when I finally straightened out my heart, awakened my companions, and stood once again before Morpheus, it wasn't just anger I felt...
It was also a deep, deep sorrow.
In that moment, we looked each other in the eyes. It hit me. My power was frail no longer; I had my friends by my side.
While she stood by herself, alone as ever, with naught but creations that bent to her will and whim.
She was the dreamspinner. She knew better than all of us how nothing in the land of dreams was real.
And yet she still chose to lose herself in such dreams. Surely, she must have had her reasons?
Hah-hahahahah-hah! You're a heartless monster!
No wonder your parents don't want you!
You'll never live through the winter!
No! You're lying!
Get lost, all of you!!
Uh-oh—run!
Morpheus, are you alright?!
Oh, Iris, is it—is it true what they said?
They're naughty children, right? The fairies won't bring sweet dreams to them, right?
I'm not a heartless monster, and my parents didn't abandon me, and I can live through the winter, right?!
Morpheus...
Oh, Iris, answer me!!
There are reasons that Morpheus cannot live outside the Castle of Dreams.
There is no storybook beauty in these lands. What Morpheus had to endure left her unable to place her trust in the sweet dreams of fairytales.
And I know full well that the Castle's sweet dreams hardly solve every problem...
'In the face of reality, what we can accomplish is so very limited.'
'It is but one thing. We nestle a seed deep in the depths of a child's heart.'
'Likely, it is what they considered the most important event, or thing, to them as children.'
'It usually lies quietly deep within. But at crucial moments—when people are at their weakest—it gives them strength.'
These words...
Are the very words you spoke to me in my dream, Miss Iris. Now, I offer these same words back to you.
I venture that perhaps, Morpheus is awaiting a dream that lets her become 'grown-up', once and for all.
As the moon rises, the doll takes on life—see, have the moons not risen?
Iris looks outside. Pale and gentle moonlight shines down on the two of them through the castle window.
Yes. You're right.
Furthermore, Miss Iris, I do have a request...
Pray tell. I'll do what's in my power.
The five of us have awakened from our dreams, but we don't seem to have returned to our own world...
Being the Master of the Castle of Dreams, I was wondering if you might know a method by which we can go back?
I happened to hear them describe this 'world' of yours while you were unconscious. From what I could gather, it certainly doesn't sound like any city on Terra.
But I've never happened across a case like yours before, and a full trawl of the Castle's archives turned up nothing either.
I intend to contact Rhodes Island—the Doctor there is quite learned, and should know a way to send you back.
Rhodes Island... the pharmaceutical company?
Indeed. You dreamt of it?
Yes. I'd be keen on visiting again, and I have some 'old friends' I'd like to meet while I'm... um...
Is something the matter?
I... I'm starting to forget what they looked like.
Iris gently takes Sakiko's hand, as though anticipating her confusion.
Miss Sakiko. You've been through so many people's dreams. I'm sure you know the Castle's rules through and through by now.
As with all dreams, the memories will begin to blur and fade from the moment you wake up.
Someday or other, you'll return to your 'world', and when you do, you'll forget about me, about the Castle's sweet dreams, about Terra itself.
How could I...!
But what happens here will not simply be for naught.
Perhaps you and your friends will have to confront those recurring problems when you go back... but I trust you'll prevail.
Even if you forget everything that happened here, you'll remember the courage you showed in times of hardship.
Yes. I trust we will too...
And when we return to our own world, Ave Mujica will remain together as Ave Mujica.
And I, Master of the Castle of Dreams, will forever remember you as you are now, as 'children'.
Perhaps I'll show up again one day, when you're all grown up—and when I do, I hope you and your friends will still be writing the story of your lives through music.
We have a promise!
What is that noise outside the door...?
We don't have another troublemaker, do we?!
Sakiko hurries to open the door.
Saki-chan—you're awake! Are you feeling okay?
(Half-conscious chirping)
Erm, I'm fine... Can you explain what all the noise out here is about?
We're setting up a new stage, and Miss Bena's making Morpheus use these little creatures to carry stuff around...
A new stage?
Yeah. Who knows how long we'll be here for. Might as well rehearse while we've got the chance.
There are so many songs I still want to work on.
Morpheus wanted to build the stage in a dream, but we and Miss Bena unanimously shot that down.
Yeah, I'll pass on dreams for the next while...
Agreed...
Won't somebody show me a smidge of concern? Why am I being given hard labor?!
(Angry chirping)
Bena, accompanying Morpheus, offers no words, instead simply giving one of Morpheus' fluffy ears a nice hard flick.
Ahkh—ow-ow-ow!
(Pained chirping)
You did the crime, now you do the time, Morpheus...
Oh, you're in this with me, Wolff! Go fit that last light!
I... alright.
Wolff places its hands on the ground, and in a snap, transforms before everyone's eyes into a bestial shape.
With the light's power cord held gently in its jaws, it leaps up to the top shelf, and effortlessly drapes it over.
Well? How's that for a new stage?
It's so pretty—!
Nyamu giddily leaps onstage and darts around set after elaborate set.
Humph. Hardly a challenge for me.
Are you forgetting the most important part? The instruments!
Ighk—stop—flicking my ears, I'll bring them out now!
Each instrument appears on stage while Morpheus is still shielding her ears, and the band takes their places.
Mm. Still feels good in the hands.
(Here's hoping that dream-long blank I had didn't screw up my muscle memory—)
Sakiko, we're all ready. Come on.
Sakiko looks up to see her companions on stage, the spotlight shining on each one of them, as well as a familiar keyboard that seems to call for her return.
I look forward to your performance, Miss Sakiko.
We missed out on seeing the whole thing last time! Now we can make up for that.
(Joyful chirping)
Getting to attend your performance, not as an enemy, but as a friend, makes me happier than anything.
Oh, you people are such goody-two-shoes!
A—Anyhow... I'll be listening closely.
Pft... hahahaha...!
Wh-What are you laughing at? Don't think you can walk all over me just because Iris and Bena are here...
No, nothing... haha! I'm coming now.
Sakiko takes her bandmates' hands, and steps onto the stage of Ave Mujica.
Much as when the last piece of a puzzle falls in place, the picture comes as no particular surprise, but it is the very moment when everything becomes whole again.
Everyone! Upon this land, we shall leave our sound!
In the moonlight, the doll takes on a temporal life.
Beneath their marionette forms, the girls' secrets lie.
Love. Fear. Sorrow. Death. Oblivion.
In a castle of dreams, secrets and sins join together and come alive once more.
Raise your candle high. Be brave through the thorns. Part this fog.
Here, a new fairytale is born.
This is the wondrous adventure story of us, the dolls.
Welcome... to the world of Ave Mujica.
